title: About Health Promotion and Chronic Disease Prevention in Canada: Research, Policy and Practice - Canada.ca
content:
## Health Promotion and Chronic Disease Prevention in Canada: Research, Policy and Practice
- Information for authors

## About Health Promotion and Chronic Disease Prevention in Canada: Research, Policy and Practice
A publication of the Public Health Agency of Canada
Published by authority of the Minister of Health. © His Majesty the King in Right of Canada, 2026 ISSN 2368-738X (Online)
Health Promotion and Chronic Disease Prevention in Canada: Research, Policy and Practice (the HPCDP Journal) is a bilingual, peer-reviewed scientific journal published and funded by the Public Health Agency of Canada’s Health Promotion and Chronic Disease Prevention Branch. The journal is open access and does not charge article processing fees.
The Journal provides accessible, high-quality evidence to advance our understanding of chronic diseases, injuries, life course health and health equity for application of disease prevention, health promotion and disparity reduction in public health.
The HPCDP Journal welcomes articles on Canadian-based or -focussed research, studies using Canadian data, or those relevant to the health of Canadians. International submissions will only be considered if they include Canadian data, results (e.g. as part of multi-country studies) and/or evidence-based discussion of implications for public health in Canada.
It is also committed to the use of sex- and  gender-based analysis in health research. Additional priority areas include mental  health; healthy living; Indigenous health; population health interventions; and  environmental health.
As a monthly publication, the Journal provides timely scientific reporting and includes content from Agency, governmental and non-governmental contributors. It is an authoritative knowledge platform for researchers, public health practitioners, health policy planners, community professionals, health educators and students.
All views and opinions expressed in Health Promotion and Chronic  Disease Prevention in Canada articles are those of the authors and do not  necessarily reflect those of the Public Health Agency of Canada or the  Government of Canada.
- 2025 CiteScore: 5.4 (Scopus)
- 2024 5-year Impact Factor: 2.9 (Web of Science)
- Among top quartile of open access public health journals (SCImago)
- Indexed in Index Medicus/MEDLINE, DOAJ, Scopus, SciSearch®, Journal Citation Reports/Science Edition and ProQuest
- Archived in PubMed Central and Government of Canada Publications Catalogue
